notes: Realistic test prompts a frontend engineer would actually type, covering the main scenarios the skill is meant to help with. Run via skill-creator's eval loop when you want quantitative validation.

evals.name: audit-existing-e2e-for-antipatterns
evals.expected_output: Identifies: (1) hard waitForTimeout anti-pattern, (2) non-web-first isVisible/expect pattern, (3) networkidle deprecated-ish, (4) conditional assertion, (5) tag embedded in title instead of { tag: [...] }, (6) BDD ID in title. Suggests web-first replacements and fixture extraction.

evals.name: design-state-based-suite-for-new-component
evals.prompt: I'm writing tests for a new <Pagination currentPage totalPages onChange isLoading /> component. It shows prev/next buttons (disabled at page 1 and last page respectively), page number buttons, and a loading skeleton when isLoading=true. Help me structure the test suite.
evals.expected_output: Proposes one test per meaningful state: (loading), (page 1 — prev disabled), (last page — next disabled), (middle page — both enabled), (click prev/next invokes onChange). Uses getByRole('button', { name: /previous/i }), not getByTestId. Doesn't suggest snapshots. May propose data-* attributes for test contracts if needed.

evals.name: playwright-flaky-test-diagnosis
evals.expected_output: Identifies that textContent+expect is a snapshot, not web-first. Suggests `await expect(page.getByTestId('result')).toContainText('Success')` for auto-retry. Optionally mentions trace/video for deeper debugging.

evals.name: layer-policy-decision
evals.prompt: Should I write a Playwright E2E test for a new <Dropdown> component I'm building? It opens/closes on click, supports arrow-key navigation, and fires onChange with the selected value.
evals.expected_output: Applies layer policy: most of this is RTL's job (component render + state + keyboard interaction). E2E only if the dropdown interacts with real browser behavior like iframe, portal positioning with real layout, or real scroll-lock. Suggests RTL unit tests for state transitions + keyboard handling, covered by userEvent.keyboard.

evals.name: jest-dom-tohaveattribute-regex-trap
evals.prompt: In my vitest + RTL test I'm asserting `expect(link).toHaveAttribute('rel', /noopener/)` but it keeps failing even though I can see rel="noopener noreferrer" in the rendered DOM. What's going on?
evals.expected_output: Identifies that jest-dom's toHaveAttribute takes a string for exact match, NOT a regex (unlike Playwright's version). Suggests either `toHaveAttribute('rel', 'noopener noreferrer')` for exact match or `expect(link.getAttribute('rel')).toMatch(/noopener/)` for regex.

evals.name: should-use-getbytestid-or-getbyrole
evals.prompt: I've been using `screen.getByTestId('submit-btn')` everywhere in my RTL tests. My teammate says I should use getByRole instead. Is that right?
evals.expected_output: Confirms teammate is right. Explains query priority: getByRole with name option is preferred because it tests what all users (including assistive tech) experience. getByTestId is last resort. Shows before/after example. Notes Playwright is slightly more tolerant of getByTestId but same priority applies.
